Travel like you
already know
the place.

Chapter Two. The problem

Every city
has two
versions.

First day of your trip.

You open a familiar app. You pick the place with 4.6 stars and 3,000 reviews. It's fine. It's also surrounded by other people who made the same choice.

Skip it.

Two streets away, there's a table the locals actually eat at. Sonder lets you know.

Chosen,
not collected.

Most of what you find online got there because enough people clicked on it. This is different.

01 01 / 03

Local knowledge

You walk in feeling like you already belong.

Every recommendation is built from what residents say to each other, in their own language. Not reviews written for tourists. The places locals actually go.

02 02 / 03

Nothing wasted

You never arrive somewhere that's closed.

Every entry is live-checked before it reaches you. No cold journeys to shuttered doors. No trust destroyed on day one.

03 03 / 03

Made for you

The list feels like it was made for you. Because it was.

A short questionnaire at the start. Travel style, budget, pace, who you're with. What you get back is a short list that fits how you actually travel.

Paris

On trust

The difference between a recommendation
and a guess is the person
standing behind it.

Anyone can surface a list. What makes it useful is judgment — knowing not just that a place is good, but why it's right for you.

Chapter Four. Where we stand

A guide you trust
has to earn that.

I

Nothing that isn't open.

Every entry is live-checked. If a place closes, it disappears before you ever see it.

II

The curation is not for sale.

No ads. No featured placements. No venue has paid to be here. We earn affiliate commission on bookings you choose to make.

III

Deliberately incomplete.

A city has thousands of places. A good guide has dozens. Every rejection makes the list sharper.

IV

Taste is the product.

Anyone can build a list. What makes this useful is the judgment behind it. Every entry is chosen by someone who has been there, knows what makes it worth your time, and would recommend it to a friend.

Central Europe
Chapter Five. In the city

Built for
the street,
not the desk.

Most guides are designed for the planning stage. Sonder is built for the moment you're already standing somewhere. Phone out, no signal, no time to research.

01

Works offline, completely

Your itinerary and all curated entries are stored on your phone before you leave. No roaming charges. No spinning loaders. No dead zones.

02

Notices when it matters

Walk past somewhere that matches your tastes. Sonder notices and tells you quietly. Not every five minutes. Only when it's worth stopping.

03

When things change

Closures, updated hours, a place that's gotten too popular. Changes surface in real time when you're connected. Alternatives surface when you're not.

04

Stays out of the way

You're in a city to be in the city. The companion is there when you need it and invisible when you don't.

Chapter Six. The promise

The guide you wish
someone had
given you.

In cities that reward people willing to look properly, Sonder is how you look properly.
